Core source is a helper library that dynamically scans caller-selected external Python packages and calls FastMCP.tool(name=...)(func) in src/mcp_auto_register/register.py:50 and :94. The scipy example has fixed source-level filter ['eigh', 'inv'], so those two example-server tools are recorded with external-handler notes; nba_api example registers a dynamic external class set and was not enumerated.
server.py creates FastMCP('Demo') and runs it over SSE; only @mcp.tool decorators were extracted, resources/prompts were ignored.

index.ts registers ListToolsRequestSchema/CallToolRequestSchema. The branch for github_hello_tool is not exposed because src/tools.ts tools array only contains three GitHub/jsonresume tools, so it was not recorded.

Swift stdio bridge exposes a baked-in tools/list snapshot (49 tools) and proxies live JSON-RPC to a local Nemeton HTTP app. No per-tool Swift handler exists in the clone; handler_resolution notes the proxy boundary.
weather_tool_server.py creates FastMCP('weather') and runs stdio; two weather tools were extracted.
src/tools/index.ts returns SumTool and PingTool. Resources/prompts exist but were not recorded as tools.

Default runtime exposes TOOL_DEFINITIONS for UNIVERSAL + lists + workspace members (registry.ts:78-86, 103-111). Legacy resource-specific configs are present but gated behind DISABLE_UNIVERSAL_TOOLS=true, so they are not included in this default tools/list extraction.

argus/mcp/server.py defines serve_mcp() and registers FastMCP tools inside it. Admin health/budget/provider/cookie tools are conditionally exposed when transport is stdio (the default) or remote auth is enabled.
server.py creates FastMCP('Kiseki-Labs-Readwise-MCP') and registers eight Readwise tools. The greeting resource at lines 254-258 was ignored.
Clone contains README.md and git metadata only; no MCP server source file or tool registration was present.

Full clone present. server.py is a hand-written JSON-RPC/FastAPI server, not official MCP SDK. It exposes TOOLS via list_tools and execute methods plus /mcp websocket/http wrappers; the concrete calculator tool is sourced from CalculatorTool and TOOLS.
Manifest clone_status failed:128; clone_detail says GitHub HTTPS could not read Username. No full source directory exists under full_repos_151_300, so no MCP tools could be verified from source.
Standard TypeScript MCP SDK Server with ListToolsRequestSchema/CallToolRequestSchema. Only active tool in ListTools is GEMINI_DEEPSEEK_SEQUENTIAL_TOOL; other candidate tools are commented out.
Standard TypeScript/CommonJS MCP SDK Server. Prompts also exist but are not tools and were not extracted.
Demo MCP server; it is still a real stdio MCP server with one registered McpServer.tool returning an image block.

Manifest clone_status failed:128 because destination path already existed and was not empty. Directory contains only a partial .git/shallow.lock-style skeleton and no working tree source files, so no MCP tools could be verified.
Standard TypeScript MCP SDK McpServer. index.js registers search/register/memory/blob/parse/feedback/browse/commons modules; every server.tool call in those registered modules was extracted.
Go project implements a custom HTTP MCP-like server with /v1/discover and /v1/call-tool rather than the official JSON-RPC method names. Tools are real provider-discovered and dispatchable handlers.

Source is an Express REST API for Monad wallet routes. Searches found no @modelcontextprotocol SDK, FastMCP, tools/list/tools/call JSON-RPC handler, server.tool/registerTool, or comparable MCP tool registration. README/package naming says MCP, but code exposes ordinary REST endpoints only.

FastMCP TypeScript server. src/index.ts imports and invokes 20 add* functions, each calling server.addTool. dist duplicates compiled output and was ignored in favor of src. makeFlwRequest.ts also contains an unused named addFlw duplicate with the same name; only the default export called from src/index.ts was recorded.
FastMCP server in src/mcp_research/server.py registers 8 concrete research tools with @server.tool decorators.

Reviewed src/index.ts and supporting routes. This is a custom HTTP/SSE JSON-RPC context service with /context routes and /capabilities, but no source-level MCP tools/list or concrete tool registration.

Outer MCP server registers one concrete tool, create_mcp_server. The embedded exampleProjectStructure string contains a template create_note tool, but that is template text, not a live tool registered by this repo.
Rust anet_mcp_server registers six concrete SQLite tools through ServerBuilder.add_tool in src/main.rs.

Reviewed src/lambdas/mcp/sse.ts and registration types. The MCP server dynamically registers tools from DynamoDB registration records (registration.name/parameters/lambdaArn). No concrete source-level tool names are present in the repo, so no tools are recorded.
Same Lambda MCP dynamic-registration implementation as repo 175. Source registers whatever tool records exist in DynamoDB, but no concrete tool definitions are present in repo source.
FastMCP server in src/index.ts registers five concrete tools, four filesystem tools and one debug listTools tool.
FastMCP app registers all tools through powerbi_mcp.tools.register_tools(mcp, ...). Found 12 concrete Power BI tools.
Main package is an evaluator/metrics library, not an MCP tool server. The example-server directory contains a real demo MCP server registering one add tool, so it is recorded.
FastMCP server factory in src/binaryninja_mcp/server.py registers 16 concrete Binary Ninja analysis tools. Resource handlers were not counted as tools.
McpServer created in src/index.ts; tools are local objects from src/tools/debates.ts and src/tools/advisor.ts registered via server.tool loops at src/index.ts:84-90.

Python MCP server lazily registers list_tools/call_tool decorators in run/main. Default TOOL_SCHEMAS contains 69 source-defined tools; ts_extended is conditionally appended under ultra profile. Profile/env filters may hide advertised tools at runtime, but handlers remain source-locatable.

Full source includes a generic MCP server that builds tools from YAML API config. Recorded the default public/apis.yaml entries because src/index.ts loads that file when no path is supplied; arbitrary user-supplied config can create additional tools not statically enumerable.
Spring AI MCP WebMVC server; EmailService bean exposes @Tool method through MethodToolCallbackProvider.
FastMCP server with five @mcp.tool() decorated async functions in src/whatsapp_mcp/server.py.

Has @modelcontextprotocol/sdk dependency, but src/server.ts is an ordinary Express HTTP API with /balance and /block routes; no MCP Server, tools/list, tools/call, server.tool, or request handlers found.
Clone contains only README.md plus .git metadata; no MCP server source files or tool registration.
Clone contains README/docs tutorial material only. The docs mention mcp_query text, but there is no local MCP server source or tool registration.
TypeScript McpServer with six direct server.tool registrations in src/index.ts.
Low-level TypeScript MCP Server. Static Tool objects are returned by ListToolsRequestSchema; CallToolRequestSchema dispatches matching switch cases.
Next.js catalog UI over public/servers.json; no local MCP server tool registration/handler found. README/catalog entries not extracted.
Python FastMCP server in zephyr/zephyr.py; one @mcp.tool handler.
Go stdio-to-remote MCP proxy/transport client. It forwards JSON-RPC to a remote server and has test mock tools, but no repo-owned MCP tool registration/handler.

Full clone contains actual MCP server under landing/mcp-src. createMcpServer registers getAvailableTools(... NEON_TOOLS ...) via server.tool at landing/mcp-src/server/index.ts:95-108; handlers resolved through NEON_HANDLERS in landing/mcp-src/tools/tools.ts.
Clone contains README.md only; no source files with MCP registration/handler.
TypeScript McpServer with two server.tool registrations in src/index.ts.

Project is an OpenAPI-to-MCP generator. MCP ListTools/CallTool code is in Jinja template openapi_to_mcp/templates/src/server.ts.j2 and generated runtime, not a concrete repo-owned tool surface; no specific generated tools present to extract.
Clone directory contains only .git metadata/shallow.lock and no working tree source files; no tools extracted.
TypeScript low-level MCP Server exposes TOOLS through ListToolsRequestSchema at src/index.ts:659 and dispatches CallToolRequestSchema to handleTool switch at src/index.ts:660-663.
Python FastMCP server factory in quack/server.py; quack.py creates server via create_server() and runs stdio/SSE.
Confirmed real TypeScript MCP server tool registrations via local registerTool wrappers called from src/tools/index.ts; README/examples ignored.
Confirmed Spring AI MCP tool exposed through ToolCallbackProvider(MethodToolCallbackProvider.toolObjects) and @Tool method.
Source is an Express REST service using a ModelContextProtocol client/object; no MCP server tools/list, tools/call, server.tool, @Tool, or equivalent registration found outside dependencies.
Confirmed FastMCP SSE server with four @mcp.tool decorated functions in main.py.

Confirmed dynamic FastMCP registration: server_lifespan calls _set_mcp_tools, converting Serena Tool classes into MCPTool entries. The exact exposed set depends on config/context/modes, so all concrete source Tool classes with apply() are recorded; tests and hooks ignored.
Confirmed low-level Python MCP Server with @app.list_tools/@app.call_tool; one Tool definition is returned from get_tool_definitions and dispatched by call_tool.
Confirmed FastMCP server with eight @mcp.tool decorated functions; @mcp.resource entries are not counted.
Confirmed low-level TypeScript MCP Server with ListToolsRequestSchema and CallToolRequestSchema handlers defining three tools.
Demo repo has a real FastMCP server registration, so the decorated tool is recorded.
Confirmed custom C++ MCP server overrides tools/list and tools/call, loading PLUGIN_TYPE_TOOLS plugins. Prompt/resource plugins are not counted.
Clone working tree contains README/license only; no MCP server source or tool registration found.
Haskell app entry point and Cabal package reference external/local PMS packages; no concrete MCP tools/list or tool definitions are present in this clone to extract.
Python MCP SDK Server in src/mcp_server_openai/server.py uses @server.list_tools and @server.call_tool; one ask-openai tool.

NCP MCP server in src/server/mcp-server.ts registers ListToolsRequestSchema/CallToolRequestSchema. getToolDefinitions returns find plus either run (default find-and-run) or code (find-and-code) depending on enableCodeMode; switch handler supports find/run/code. Test dummy/mock MCP servers ignored.
Two Python FastMCP demo servers under servers/calculator and servers/local; all @mcp.tool handlers recorded.

TypeScript McpServer in src/index.ts registers 13 tools exported from src/tools/*.ts with mcp.registerTool(name, config, init(...)). Zod schemas recorded as refs/raw, not expanded beyond located source refs.
Go mcp-go server adds Skopeo tools via AddTools(slices.Concat(...)) in pkg/mcp/mcp.go:28; tools defined with mcp.NewTool in pkg/mcp/skopeo.go.
Python FastMCP server in src/mac_monitor/monitor.py registers three @mcp.tool process/system monitoring handlers.

TypeScript SDK Server in src/index.ts returns eight tools in ListToolsRequestSchema and dispatches them in CallToolRequestSchema to CoreTools/TimeTools. Additional call branches not present in tools/list were not extracted.

Laravel/Superconductor MCP server registers tools in routes/tools.php via MCP::tool and exposes the same names in VibesServer::$tools. Tool handler classes use #[ToolCall(...)] attributes and handle() methods.
TypeScript McpServer stdio server in src/index.ts registers three tools with server.registerTool; schemas are Zod .shape refs from src/config/schemas.ts.
Full clone contains README/LICENSE only plus git metadata; no local MCP server source or tool registration/handler to extract.
TypeScript low-level SDK server in src/index.ts advertises five tools through ListToolsRequestSchema and dispatches them in CallToolRequestSchema switch.
Minimal line-delimited JSON MCP-like server in server.py returns one tool for tools/list and handles tools/call by running an external sync.py script under ~/agent-factory.
Creates and starts an mcp-framework HTTP server in src/index.ts, but no repo-owned concrete tools are imported or registered; commented WeatherTool import ignored.

Unity integration includes a Node MCP server under mcpServer/src. registerTools advertises 15 tools via ListToolsRequestSchema at toolDefinitions.ts:75 and dispatches Unity/WebSocket or filesystem handlers in CallToolRequestSchema.
Python FastMCP server in src/ilograph_mcp/server.py creates the server and calls register_* functions that define 11 @mcp.tool-decorated handlers.
TypeScript server in src/mcpServer.ts creates an MCP Server and registers five crypto daemon tools with server.tool; resources/prompts ignored.
Python MCP server in src/relayshield_mcp/server.py uses @app.list_tools to return nine types.Tool definitions and @app.call_tool to dispatch to RelayShield API endpoints.

Local stdio entry point advertises snapshotted data/tools.json and proxies every CallToolRequest to hosted REMOET_MCP_URL; hosted server remains source of execution truth. Per instructions, remote catalog/proxy tool surface not extracted as repo-owned handlers.
FastMCP server in beehiiv_server.py registers five @mcp.tool handlers and runs via mcp.run().
Full clone contains three concrete geocoder MCP server implementations: python-googlemaps, python-urllib, and typescript-googlemaps. mcp-client and test caller files ignored.
TypeScript McpServer in src/index.ts creates server, calls registerTools(server), and registers four server.tool handlers.
Low-level Python MCP Server in src/age_mcp_server/server.py registers tools via @server.list_tools and dispatches execution in @server.call_tool.
TypeScript server in src/server.mjs registers three server.registerTool handlers; resources/prompts are not extracted as tools.

Retry clone is present. kubectl_mcp_tool/mcp_server.py initializes FastMCP and setup_tools() calls register_*_tools functions; extracted @mcp.tool handlers from kubectl_mcp_tool/tools/*.py. Optional browser/UI tools are source-registered behind availability checks and are included because they are real server registration handlers.

mcp-server/src/server/server.ts creates shared McpServer; mcp-server/src/index.ts imports transaction modules for side-effect registration. Extracted server.registerTool handlers from mcp-server/src/transactions/**/*.ts.
No full clone directory found under full_repos_151_300 for xx238xx--rt96-hub__prompt-tester; candidate hints show clone failed:128. Per instruction, repo record retained with empty tool array.
FastMCP server in src/mcp_pkm_logseq/server.py registers three @mcp.tool handlers. The logseq:// resource is not extracted as a tool.
Directory exists after retry but contains only .git metadata/no readable working tree source files. Per instruction, repo record retained with empty tool array.
Full clone is present but contains README.md plus git metadata only; no MCP server source or tool registration was found.

src/stela_mcp/server.py registers low-level @self.server.list_tools() and @self.server.call_tool() handlers. list_tools_impl returns 14 static filesystem/shell tools; call_tool dispatches by tool_name to same-named LocalSystemServer methods.
Full clone contains README.md plus git metadata only; no MCP server source or tool registration was found.

src/mcp_github/issues_pr_analyser.py dynamically registers GitHubIntegration methods that have _mcp_annotations. Only decorated source methods were recorded; FastMCP provider apps/resources/skills were not treated as tools.
coremcp.py creates FastMCP("Prokerala MCP") and has 16 @mcp.tool decorators. The duplicate exposed name get_panchang at coremcp.py:271 was skipped, leaving 15 distinct tool names.
index.js is an Express/OpenAI HTTP endpoint at /mcp/context, not an MCP protocol server with tools/list or registered MCP tools; no MCP tools recorded.
Full clone contains README.md plus git metadata only; no MCP server source or tool registration was found.

Manifest clone_status is failed:128 because destination already existed, but the source directory contains index.js/server-http.js/package files and was reviewed. index.js exposes two tools through ListToolsRequestSchema and handles both in CallToolRequestSchema; duplicate HTTP server registrations were not double-counted.

src/server.ts creates an McpServer and registers each Tool from src/tools/index.ts with server.registerTool. Two concrete Tool classes are exported and recorded. Remote catalog/server.json entries were ignored.

package.json main points to src/mcp/server.js. That stdio MCP server registers 51 tools via server.tool(...). src/mcp/http-server.js exposes a smaller remote subset and was not double-counted; public HTTP API endpoints and docs were ignored.

Confirmed TypeScript McpServer.registerTool registrations via registerAllTools called from createMcpServer; README/tests ignored. Candidate hints undercounted tourism.ts; full source has five tourism tools plus search and geojson.
Confirmed Go mcp-go server: main.go calls s.AddTools with five ServerTool values; each tool definition is mcp.NewTool and handler is the paired handle*Request function.
Full clone contains only README.md and git metadata; README describes a remote MCP endpoint but no source-confirmed MCP server tool registration/handler is present.

Current full clone exists. Confirmed low-level TypeScript MCP Server with ListToolsRequestSchema returning getMcpToolsList() and CallToolRequestSchema dispatching handleCustomToolCall over customTools; prior failed hint ignored.

Current full clone exists. Confirmed provider-loaded Google Chat FastMCP instance; src/providers/google_chat/tools/__init__.py imports tool modules, and custom @tool() decorator wraps mcp_instance.tool() plus registry registration. Commented-out tools and tests/docs ignored.
Confirmed TypeScript McpServer.tool registrations in src/index.ts backed by tool objects in src/tools; README ignored.
Confirmed FastMCP server with @mcp.tool decorated functions in server.py. README tool count is not used; only source decorators recorded.

Confirmed package CLI imports GreptileMCPServer from src/server.ts; ListToolsRequestSchema returns five tools and CallToolRequestSchema dispatches the same names. src/index.ts and src/smithery.ts contain overlapping platform/export implementations, so they were not double-counted; resources/prompts ignored.
Confirmed Python MCP FastMCP server with @mcp.tool decorated functions in codemagic_mcp/server.py; README ignored.

Confirmed low-level JavaScript MCP Server: both stdio and HTTP classes return shared TOOL_DEFINITIONS via ListToolsRequestSchema and dispatch names through CallToolRequestSchema. Shared definitions recorded once; transport duplicate not double-counted.
